Telangana Revolt against the Nizam

# Telangana Revolt against the Nizam

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Jul 14, 2022

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

The Telangana Rebellion between 1946 to 1951, was one of the most intense uprisings against the Nizam rule. This is different from the Telangana movement that came much later.

For a long time, the rural parts of Hyderabad State, were divided into what was called as Samsthanams, essentially pieces of feudal territories, under the oppressive rule of the Reddy, Velama doras in Telangana, the Deshmukhs in other regions, who ran a brutal and often oppressive rule. They owned most of the land, and collected the taxes from the poor peasantry, keeping them in perpetual bonded labor( called as Vetti Chakiri). These landlords were the masters of their own land, and had a good bonding with the Nizam and his nobles. The Nizam on the other hand, hardly had any control over these lands, and let the Doras run it as per their own free whims, it was pretty much a quid pro quo arrangement.

History Under Your Feet is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!9qtr!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F15fc2291-ee1b-4d72-a41e-69d8b41d1e58_209x241.png)

![r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!9qtr!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F15fc2291-ee1b-4d72-a41e-69d8b41d1e58_209x241.png "r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

This frail looking old woman in the picture, was the one who sparked off the uprising against the feudal landlords in Hyderabad State. Chakali Illamma, was the Rosa Parks of this movement, her defiance sparking a revolt. Belonging to a backward Rajaka community, Illama defied the local zamindar Ramachandra Reddy when he forcibly tried to occupy her 4 acres of land. That act of defiance, lit the spark, and soon many began to resist the tyranny of the Doras.

Taking advantage of this, the Communists jumped into the fray, and soon took over the movement. The revolt against the Telangana doras, that essentially was against bonded labor and exploitation, saw the Nizam himself become the target. To the villagers and activists, the exploitative landlords, were essentially stooges of the Nizam himself.One of the more popular revolutionary songs of that period was *“Bandenaka Bandi Katti, Padaharu Ballu Katti, Nenosthanu koduka ra Nizam sarakaroda”.* Loosely translated it means *“Tying one cart after another, and 16 carts in tow, I am coming for you stooge of the Nizam”.* Rural discontent was high against the Nizam, on one side the Communist leaders like Sundarayya, Ch.Rajeswara Rao , on the other side, Congress leaders like Swami Ramananda Teertha, P.V.Narasimha Rao, rising up against his rule.

It was a successful agitation by the Communists, as they took control over 3000 villages and distributed around 10,000 acres of land to the peasants. It was a bloody revolution against the combined might of the Nizam and the private armies of the Doras. Around 4000 peasants lost their lives in the struggle, but they did not flinch from their resolve. The peasants main demand was to end the system of Vetti Chakiri( bonded labor) and do away with all the fake debts in their name.

It was this revolt, that prompted the Nizam to use the dreaded Razakars, to counter the agitators. The Razakars, were the Nizam’s own storm troopers, running a vicious campaign of massacre, looting and rape in the villages of Telangana. Many people in the villages ran away into the forests or abandoned forts to escape the atrocities of the Razakars. The Communist parties however trained the peasants to use guerilla tactics against the Razakars. The Doras too were either driven out or killed, and and Soviet style communes were established in the liberated villages.

Some of the leading lights of this movement were

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eJyQ!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9ad5053e-1240-4c22-917d-b20833bead9d_275x183.png)

![r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eJyQ!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9ad5053e-1240-4c22-917d-b20833bead9d_275x183.png "r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam")

Raavi Narayana Reddy, one of the founders of the CPI, and also the first Communist MP in Lok Sabha.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZLPA!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6674dc90-cf26-4903-8931-546ff2e79792_178x224.png)

![r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZLPA!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6674dc90-cf26-4903-8931-546ff2e79792_178x224.png "r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam")

Puchalapalli Sundarayya one of the founders of the CPM, took active part in the revolt, as well as a noted philanthropist.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!5esp!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F188a66ee-5d9a-4038-8a5c-df8da442487b_318x244.png)

![r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!5esp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F188a66ee-5d9a-4038-8a5c-df8da442487b_318x244.png "r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Suddala Hanumanthu who inspired the masses with his revolutionary poems. His folk song Palletoori Pillagada( The Village Guy) exhorted the masses to revolt against the oppression. His son Suddala Ashoka Teja is a well known lyricist in Telugu cinema.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0GBD!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc98095ab-b1eb-4a73-bd23-153a4ecad704_572x422.png)

![r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0GBD!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc98095ab-b1eb-4a73-bd23-153a4ecad704_572x422.png "r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Chandra Rajeswara Rao, CPI leader.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!erII!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8c574bc5-fad5-454e-a081-fe3d53e398ff_140x200.png)

![r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!erII!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8c574bc5-fad5-454e-a081-fe3d53e398ff_140x200.png "r/IndiaSpeaks - Telangana Revolt against the Nizam")

Makhdoom Mohiuddin, again like Suddala used poetry to ignite the spirit of the masses.

This revolt in a way was the proverbial Nail in the Coffin for the Nizam, and soon he was overthrown by Operation Polo.
